superseded_reason: This run specialized TileLang on dynamic route active_rows and repeatedly compiled during training; the static-route rerun is the valid e2e read.

tilelang_runtime.dependency_note: The overlay keeps the MORI/AITER TorchTitan runtime intact and adds TileLang. Docker build warned that numba 0.57.1 requires numpy<1.25, but torch, tilelang, mori, and aiter import smokes passed.

kernel_local_best_candidate.name: selected_limit_hsk_bf16_scaled_source
kernel_local_best_candidate.shape: S4096 H64 D512 layer2 actual LI topk512
kernel_local_best_candidate.read: This is the best current TileLang kernel-local sparse compressed-dKV candidate. It is not an end-to-end training number.

e2e_runs.resolution: Patched sparse_mla_shared_kv_triton_forward_backend.py to discover tools/dsv4_runtime_probe from parent directories or TORCHTITAN_DSV4_TILELANG_ADAPTER_DIR before importing the TileLang adapter.

e2e_runs.note: Completed but showed repeated TileLang compilation of mfma_panel_atomic_final_limited_bshd_source_hsk_bf16_scaled_prim during backward.
Confirmation run completed, but repeated TileLang JIT/compile messages persisted into step 3.

decision.read: The best TileLang kernel-local candidate remains promising for sparse compressed-dKV, but the current TorchTitan e2e integration is not competitive. E2E is dominated by repeated TileLang compilation/dynamic JIT behavior and possibly the current autograd call shape, so the retained e2e baseline stays the non-TileLang CE2/MORI run at 58.71 TFLOPs/GPU.
next_actions: Make the TileLang kernel object statically cached per rank/layer/backend tuple instead of recompiling during backward.
Resolve or intentionally suppress the TileLang data-race checker warning only after confirming the group_acc update pattern is race-free.
Rerun e2e after compile caching to separate actual kernel latency from JIT overhead.
If cached e2e remains slow, profile the TileLang backward call boundary to see whether packing, Python dispatch, or autograd wrapper churn dominates.
